question_text: A rigid container holds 2.4 m³ of nitrogen gas at a pressure of 150 kPa and 20°C. The container is heated to 80°C. What is the new pressure inside the container? (Assume the volume stays constant and use Gay-Lussac's Law)
explanation: Using Gay-Lussac's Law (P₁/T₁ = P₂/T₂) with temperatures in Kelvin: (150 kPa)/(293 K) = P₂/(353 K). Solving: P₂ = 150 × (353/293) ≈ 180 kPa. This demonstrates how pressure increases with temperature when volume is constant.
